skill.name: meta-rob-plot
skill.description: Draw ROB2 risk-of-bias plots, including a Traffic Light Plot and a Summary Bar Plot. Input is a CSV file with ROB2 assessments for each study; output are two PNG plot files.
skill.summary: Automates Cochrane ROB2 risk-of-bias visualization from CSV data to publication-ready plots.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 66 static findings across 5 files (996 lines). All 22 high-severity 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives - the scanner misidentified YAML front matter, markdown table content, and JSON audit metadata as cryptographic references. All 41 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings in SKILL.md are false positives - they are markdown code fence delimiters, not executable backticks. Two low-risk legitimate patterns confirmed: filesystem write operations (os.makedirs for output PNG files) and controlled external command execution (system2 calls to fallback Python script). No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent detected. Legacy audit confirms 89/100 score (Production Ready) with all veto gates passing.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write Operations
low_findings.description: Script creates output directory using os.makedirs. This is legitimate behavior for a plotting skill that writes PNG files. Output directory is user-specified, not a system location.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution via R system2
low_findings.description: R script calls Python fallback via system2 when the primary R plot generation fails. Arguments are hardcoded script parameters, not user-controlled input. Legitimate error recovery mechanism.

content.user_title: Generate ROB2 Risk-of-Bias Plots from CSV Data
content.value_statement: Create publication-ready Traffic Light Plots and Summary Bar Plots from Cochrane ROB2 risk-of-bias assessments. This skill automates visualization so researchers can focus on interpreting results instead of manual chart creation.
actual_capabilities: Read a CSV file with ROB2 domain assessments and generate a Traffic Light Plot as a PNG image
Generate a Summary Bar Plot showing risk-of-bias distribution across all domains
Validate input data columns and allowed values before generating plots
Support both Python (pandas/matplotlib) and R (ggplot2) execution paths with automatic fallback
Output a structured summary with per-domain and overall risk counts and percentages
Handle studies with missing assessment data by marking them as No information
limitations: Input data must follow the specific ROB2 column format (study, d1-d5, overall) with predefined values
Cannot perform statistical analysis beyond generating the two specified plot types
Requires R with ggplot2 and reshape2 or Python with pandas and matplotlib installed
Does not support custom color schemes or plot layout modifications

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Researcher
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a Cochrane-style systematic review needs to visualize risk-of-bias assessments for included studies.

use_cases.title: Meta-Analysis Author
use_cases.description: A meta-analysis author prepares publication figures showing the risk-of-bias distribution across studies in a review.

use_cases.title: Evidence Synthesis Student
use_cases.description: A graduate student learning evidence synthesis methods generates ROB2 plots for a course project on study quality assessment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic ROB2 Plots from CSV
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a CSV file with ROB2 risk-of-bias assessments. Generate a Traffic Light Plot and a Summary Bar Plot from the file at [path/to/data.csv].
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a correctly formatted CSV and needs default plots.

prompt_templates.title: ROB2 Plots with Custom Naming
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate ROB2 plots from [path/to/data.csv]. Use [study_name]_rob2 as the output file prefix and save the files to [path/to/output].
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to specify custom output file names and directory.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Data Before Plotting
prompt_templates.prompt: First validate the CSV file at [path/to/data.csv] to confirm it has the required columns (study, d1-d5, overall) and valid assessment values. Then generate the Traffic Light Plot and Summary Bar Plot.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to catch data format issues before plot generation.

prompt_templates.title: Full Workflow with Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the R script to generate ROB2 plots from [path/to/data.csv]. Save the output as [prefix]_rob2_light_plot.png and [prefix]_rob2_bar_plot.png in [path/to/output]. If the R script fails, use the Python fallback script automatically.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants the complete workflow with automatic error recovery.

output_examples.input: Generate ROB2 plots from the file studies_rob2.csv.
output_examples.output:
ROB2 Risk-of-Bias Plotting Completed

[Included studies] 10

[Output files]
- Traffic Light Plot: ./rob2_rob2_light_plot.png
- Summary Bar Plot: ./rob2_rob2_bar_plot.png

[Risk-of-bias summary]
Domain               Low    Some concerns    High    No info
D1 (Randomization)   8      2                0       0
D2 (Deviations)      7      3                0       0
D3 (Missing data)    9      1                0       0
D4 (Measurement)     6      4                0       0
D5 (Reporting)       8      2                0       0
Overall              5      4                1       0

[Overall assessment]
- Low risk studies: 5 (50%)
- Some concerns: 4 (40%)
- High risk studies: 1 (10%)

output_examples.input: The R script failed. Try the Python fallback.
output_examples.output:
R script encountered an error. Switching to Python fallback for plot generation.

Output files generated successfully:
- Traffic Light Plot: ./output/my_study_rob2_light_plot.png
- Summary Bar Plot: ./output/my_study_rob2_bar_plot.png

best_practices: Always validate the input CSV file format and column names before running the plotting scripts.
Use meaningful output file name prefixes to distinguish between different analysis batches.
Review the Traffic Light Plot together with the Summary Bar Plot for a complete understanding of risk distribution.
anti_patterns: Do not skip data validation; invalid values or missing columns will cause script errors and wasted execution time.
Do not use the tool to draw conclusions about study quality without consulting the full text of each study.
Do not modify the R or Python scripts without testing, as this may break the documented execution path.

faq.question: What input format does this skill require?
faq.answer: A CSV file with columns: study, d1, d2, d3, d4, d5, and overall. Each domain column must contain valid assessment values.

faq.question: What are the valid assessment values for each domain?
faq.answer: Low, Some concerns, High, and No information. These match the Cochrane ROB2 tool categories.

faq.question: What output files does this skill produce?
faq.answer: Two PNG images: a Traffic Light Plot and a Summary Bar Plot. It also prints a text summary with risk counts and percentages.

faq.question: Does this skill require Python, R, or both?
faq.answer: The primary execution path uses R with ggplot2 and reshape2. A Python fallback using pandas and matplotlib is available if R is not installed.

faq.question: Can I customize the output file names?
faq.answer: Yes. You can specify a custom file name prefix as the second argument. The default prefix is rob2.

faq.question: What happens if my data has missing values?
faq.answer: Missing values are filled with No information. The plots display them in gray and they are counted separately in the summary.
